REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT JOHN DANFORTH REVISED NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR SANCTIONS PURSUANT TO C.C.P 128.7 IS DENIED. THE "OFFENDING" DOCUMENTS WERE FILED AND SERVED ON SEPTEMBER 24, 2015 IN OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO RECLASSIFY. MOTION TO RECLASSIFY WAS HEARD ON OCTOBER 7, 2015. MOTION FOR SANCTIONS WAS SERVED ON NOVEMBER 13, 2015 AND FILED ON FEBRUARY 18, 2016. " 'THE PURPOSE OF THE SAFE HARBOR PROVISIONS IS TO PERMIT AN OFFENDING PARTY TO AVOID SANCTIONS BY WITHDRAWING THE IMPROPER PLEADING DURING THE SAFE HARBOR PERIOD. THIS PERMITS A PARTY TO WITHDRAW A QUESTIONABLE PLEADING WITHOUT PENALTY, THUS SAVING THE COURT AND THE PARTIES TIME AND MONEY LITIGATING THE PLEADINGS AS WELL AS THE SANCTIONS REQUEST.' " SEE MALOVEC V. HAMRELL (1999) 70 CAL.APP.4TH 434; BANKS V. HATHAWAY, PERRETT, WEBSTER, PWERS & CHRISTMAN (2002) 97 CAL.APP.4TH 949; LI V. MAJESTIC INDUSTRY HILLS, LLC (2009) 177 CAL.APP.4TH 585. DEFENDANT'S MOTION DOES NOT SERVE THIS PURPOSE. ALSO, THE FACTUAL DISPUTES REGARDING CONDITION OF THE PROPERTY BEFORE, DURING AND AFTER THE TENANCY FORM THE BASIS OF THIS CASE. PARTIES' INTERPRETATIONS OF THE FACTS (EVEN CONSIDERING PLAINTIFF'S CONCESSION THAT THE HEAT ISSUE WAS TIMELY RESOLVED CONTRARY TO PLAINTIFF'S ASSERTION IN DECLARATION TO THE CONTRARY) DO NOT WARRANT TERMINATING SANCTIONS URGED BY DEFENDANT, ESPECIALLY IN LIGHT OF DEFENDANT'S LACK OF DUE DILIGENCE IN SEEKING SANCTIONS FOR FILINGS MADE ON SEPTEMBER 24, 2015. CCP 128.7(C). PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II CLERK: J. MURPHY; REPORTED: JUDITH DEALBA CSR#5709 (650)571-1473, (501)

Off Calendar Jun-08-2016 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Sep-20-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Revised Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To C.C.P 128.7
Off Calendar Mar-14-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
John Danforth (650)740-1101 Defendant/Moving party on his own behalf
Dave Crow, Esq., (415)552-552-9060 of Crow & Rose for Plaintiff/Opposing party Emil Michael

Argued by counsel for both parties. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT JOHN DANFORTH Revised Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To C.C.P 128.7 is DENIED. The "offending" documents were filed and served on September 24, 2015 in opposition to motion to reclassify. Motion to reclassify was heard on October 7, 2015. Motion for sanctions was served on November 13, 2015 and filed on February 18, 2016. " 'The purpose of the safe harbor provisions is to permit an offending party to avoid sanctions by withdrawing the improper pleading during the safe harbor period. This permits a party to withdraw a questionable pleading without penalty, thus saving the court and the parties time and money litigating the pleadings as well as the sanctions request.' " See Malovec v. Hamrell (1999) 70 Cal.App.4th 434; Banks v. Hathaway, Perrett, Webster, Pwers & Christman (2002) 97 Cal.App.4th 949; Li v. Majestic Industry Hills, LLC (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 585. Defendant's motion does not serve this purpose. Also, the factual disputes regarding condition of the property before, during and after the tenancy form the basis of this case. Parties' interpretations of the facts (even considering Plaintiff's concession that the heat issue was timely resolved contrary to Plaintiff's assertion in declaration to the contrary) do not warrant terminating sanctions urged by Defendant, especially in light of Defendant's lack of due diligence in seeking sanctions for filings made on September 24, 2015. CCP 128.7(c). Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is denied. Order approved as to form and signed in Court. Judge: A. James Robertson II Clerk: J. Murphy; Reported: Judith DeAlba CSR#5709 (650)571-1473, (501)

***See Reporter's Transcripts for Complete Record***

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 128.7
Off Calendar Jan-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
John Danforth (650)740-1101 Defendant/Moving party on his own behalf
Dave Crow, Esq., (415)552-552-9060 of Crow & Rose for Plaintiff/Opposing party Emil Michael

Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: DEFENDANT JOHN DANFORTH's Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 128.7 is Taken Under Submission. The Court informs counsel that the moving party failed to comply with the safe harbor provision and failed to serve the Plaintiff with a motion for sanctions (including notice of motion, motion, points and authorities and supporting evidence) 21 days before the motion was filed. Parties' requests for sanctions are denied. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Reported: Carol Haraburda CSR# 8052 (925)360-6065. (501)
